activated_rules.rule_name: Document pairing validation passed via identifiable consignor/consignee in the invoice
Consignor found explicitly in invoice text
Commercial name vs registered company name
EU and specific EU-member-state origin equivalence
Single weight value without GW/NW requires manual review

consignor.invoice_value:
Elmo Sweden AB
SE-512 81 SVENLJUNGA
SWEDEN
consignor.motivation: The certificate consignor name 'Elmo Sweden AB' is explicitly present in the invoice footer/postal address block. Rule 4.1.0 allows consignor verification when the company name appears anywhere in the invoice text. No contradictory consignor party is shown.

consignee.certificate_value:
Boretius Objekt Nähservice 
Südring 4
76473 Iffezheim
Germany
consignee.invoice_value:
Boretius Objekt Nähservice ( 11007861 )
für Dekorationen GmbH
Südring 4
DE 76473 - Iffezheim , Germany
consignee.motivation: The certificate consignee appears in the invoice payer/ship-to block as a longer registered name: 'Boretius Objekt Nähservice ... für Dekorationen GmbH'. Street 'Südring 4', postcode '76473' and country Germany align. Under rule 4.1.3.4 as applied through section 4.2, the shorter commercial name in the certificate can be matched to the registered invoice name.

goods_description.motivation: The invoice contains the exact product designation 'ELMOSOFT 97038 I' and the accompanying text 'Upholstery Leather'. Only case normalization is needed; no semantic interpretation is required.

quantity.motivation: The invoice explicitly contains the same numeric weight value in 'Weight 4 kgs'. However, the certificate states a single weight value '4 Kgs' without GW/NW/Gross/Net category. Rule 4.4.2.2 requires MANUAL_REVIEW, not MATCH, when one uncategorized weight value is identifiable.

origin.motivation: The invoice explicitly states 'Swedish Origin'. This directly verifies the certificate's 'Swedish Origin' and also verifies 'EUROPEAN UNION' under rule 4.5.4.2 because Sweden is an EU member state.

manual_review_triggers: Quantity control point triggered rule 4.4.2.2: the certificate states only '4 Kgs' without GW/NW/Gross/Net, even though the invoice shows 'Weight 4 kgs'.
overall_assessment.decision_summary: A valid certificate-invoice pair could be identified. Consignor, consignee, goods description and origin are verifiable against the invoice. The overall result is MANUAL_REVIEW because the certificate quantity is expressed only as '4 Kgs' without weight category, which rule 4.4.2.2 does not permit for automatic MATCH.
